Core Topic: Cognitive setup: forgetting curve, spaced repetition system, active recall vs. passive reading
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: None yet — establish baseline
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write from memory: explain in plain English why passive SQL tutorials fail; no peeking
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 0: Learning system configuration
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Schedule Week 3 and Week 6 review of Ch. 1 concepts now
Notes: Configure your spaced repetition tool (Anki or flashcard deck) before writing a single query

Core Topic: Database structure, tables, rows, columns; SELECT basics
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 1 active recall notes (first spaced gap: 7 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write 3 SELECT queries from memory against a blank schema; check syntax only after
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 1: Relational model mental model
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 10 review of Week 2 syntax cards
Notes: First query writing session — resist copy-paste

Core Topic: Filtering with WHERE; comparison and logical operators (AND, OR, NOT)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 2–3 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Close the book; reconstruct the operator precedence rules; write one query using all three logical operators
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 2: Row-level filtering
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 18 review of WHERE clause flashcards
Notes: Active recall beats re-reading: test before reviewing notes

Core Topic: Sorting with ORDER BY; limiting results with LIMIT/TOP
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 1 system concepts (gap: 21 days — second spaced interval); review Ch. 4–5 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: From memory: write a query that filters, sorts descending, and limits to 10 rows — no reference
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 3: Result set shaping
Notes: Combine SELECT + WHERE + ORDER BY + LIMIT as one consolidated unit

Core Topic: Aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX); GROUP BY and HAVING
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 2–3 cards (gap: ~21 days — second interval); review Ch. 6 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write from memory: query that groups sales by category, filters groups with HAVING, orders by total descending
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 4: Data summarisation
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 32 review of aggregate syntax cards
Notes: HAVING vs. WHERE is a high-frequency active recall target

Core Topic: JOINs: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 4–5 cards (gap: ~21 days); review Ch. 7–8 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Draw the Venn diagram for each JOIN type; then write one query for each without notes
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 5: Multi-table reasoning
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Schedule Week 9 and Week 12 review of JOIN logic
Notes: JOINs are the single highest-value consolidation point in the pyramid

Core Topic: Subqueries and nested SELECT statements
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Layers 1–3 consolidated (Ch. 2–6) — scheduled from Week 4; review Ch. 9 JOIN cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write a correlated subquery from scratch; explain aloud why it re-executes per row
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 6: Query composition
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 46 review of subquery pattern cards
Notes: Active recall here: narrate your query logic before running it

Core Topic: String functions, date functions, NULL handling (IS NULL, COALESCE, NULLIF)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 7–8 aggregate cards (gap: ~21 days — second interval); review Ch. 10 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: From memory: write queries using COALESCE to handle NULLs in a join result; list 5 date functions without reference
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 7: Data cleaning and transformation
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Schedule Week 11 review of NULL and function cards
Notes: NULL behaviour is a top interview trap — prioritise active recall

Core Topic: Window functions: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG, LEAD, PARTITION BY
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 9 JOIN logic (gap: ~21 days — second interval); review Ch. 11–12 cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write a query ranking employees by salary within each department using PARTITION BY — no reference
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 8: Advanced analytical computation
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 60 review of window function pattern cards
Notes: Window functions appear in ~80% of analyst technical interviews per Ch. 16

Core Topic: CTEs (WITH clause); query optimisation and execution plans
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 10 subquery cards (gap: ~21 days); review Ch. 13 window function cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Refactor a multi-level subquery into a CTE from memory; explain one execution plan observation aloud
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 9: Readable, performant SQL
Notes: CTEs replace nested subqueries — active recall: when would you choose each?

Core Topic: Portfolio building with real datasets; gap between tutorial SQL and production SQL
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Ch. 11–12 NULL/function cards (gap: ~21 days — third interval); review Ch. 14–15 CTE cards (gap: ~10 days)
Active Recall Checkpoint: Without notes: list 3 ways production data differs from tutorial data (per Ch. 16); draft one portfolio project brief
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 10: Professional application
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Day 74 review of all interview question type cards
Notes: Begin a public GitHub SQL portfolio this week — documented analysis, not just code

Core Topic: Five interview question types; stakeholder communication; 12-week fluency maintenance schedule
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Full-pyramid spaced review: Ch. 1 system (gap: ~77 days — third interval); Ch. 9 JOINs (gap: ~42 days — third interval); all Layer cards in sequence
Active Recall Checkpoint: Answer all five SQL interview question types aloud with structured verbal responses; no notes
Progressive Skill Layer: Foundation Layer 11: Practitioner identity and compounding fluency
Forgetting Curve Intervention: Set recurring 21-day review cadence for all card decks going forward
Notes: Fluency maintenance: Ch. 16 schedule — 21-day intervals prevent forgetting curve erosion post-course

Core Topic: Second Review (Days After First Review)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Review Format
Active Recall Checkpoint: Active Recall Method
Progressive Skill Layer: Forgetting Curve Risk Without Review

Week: Ch. 1 — Cognitive system concepts (forgetting curve, spaced repetition, active recall)
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 4)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Flashcard deck — write definition from memory before flipping
Active Recall Checkpoint: Explain each concept aloud as if teaching a colleague
Progressive Skill Layer: High — abstract concepts erode fastest without reinforcement

Week: Ch. 2–3 — SELECT, table structure, relational model
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 5)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Write 3 queries from blank schema memory
Active Recall Checkpoint: Reconstruct a CREATE TABLE statement and matching SELECT
Progressive Skill Layer: Medium — syntax fades within 10 days without practice

Week: Ch. 4–5 — WHERE, AND, OR, NOT, operator precedence
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 6)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Close notes; write filter query with nested logic
Active Recall Checkpoint: Predict query output before running; check after
Progressive Skill Layer: High — operator precedence errors are a persistent interview failure point

Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 7)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Combine with WHERE in single query from memory
Active Recall Checkpoint: Explain why ORDER BY executes after WHERE logically
Progressive Skill Layer: Low-Medium — simple syntax but must be consolidated with filtering

Week: Ch. 7–8 — Aggregates (COUNT, SUM, AVG), GROUP BY, HAVING
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 8)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Write grouped summary query from memory; include HAVING
Active Recall Checkpoint: Explain HAVING vs. WHERE without notes — verbal only
Progressive Skill Layer: High — HAVING vs. WHERE confusion is the #1 aggregate mistake

Week: Ch. 9 — INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER JOIN
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 9)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Draw all four Venn diagrams; write one query per type
Active Recall Checkpoint: Narrate join logic aloud: "This returns rows where…"
Progressive Skill Layer: Very High — JOINs are the most-forgotten and most-tested SQL skill

Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 10)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: Write correlated subquery from scratch; no scaffold
Active Recall Checkpoint: Explain execution order: outer vs. inner query
Progressive Skill Layer: High — correlated subquery re-execution logic fades quickly

Week: Ch. 11–12 — String/date functions, NULL, COALESCE, NULLIF
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 11)
Spaced Repetition Review Sessions: List 5 date functions and 3 NULL-handling functions from memory
Active Recall Checkpoint: Write a query replacing NULLs in a LEFT JOIN result
Progressive Skill Layer: Medium-High — NULL behaviour is counterintuitive and interview-critical

Week: Ch. 13 — Window functions, PARTITION BY, ROW_NUMBER, LAG, LEAD
Core Topic: Day 21 (Week 12)
